headline: Hoto's PixelDrive screwdriver is down to $60, matching its best price
summary: Hoto's PixelDrive cordless screwdriver is on sale for $59.99. This matches its lowest price ever. It's ideal for furniture assembly and electronics repair.

headline: America's greatest idea is still under threat
summary: The US recently celebrated its 250th anniversary with global celebrations. The article discusses threats to America's core ideals. Nations worldwide, including France and Japan, marked the occasion.

headline: Five questions for Dr. Rubin, who's armed with a mic and a bowtie
summary: Dr. Zachary Rubin, an expert, combats online misinformation. He uses his platform to share accurate information. The article features an interview with him.

headline: Former Xbox studios Double Fine and Compulsion will keep games after going indie
summary: Microsoft is spinning off four Xbox studios, including Double Fine and Compulsion. Double Fine and Compulsion will retain their game franchises. This is part of a larger restructuring announced by Xbox.

headline: U.S. Helps Vietnam Arrest 7 People Allegedly Involved in Popular Free Anime Streaming Site
summary: HiAnime, a major free anime streaming site, recently shut down. Seven individuals linked to the site were arrested in Vietnam. The arrests were made with assistance from the U.S.

headline: Pokémon Go Hosting Major 10th Anniversary Broadcast This Week
summary: Pokémon Go is celebrating its 10th anniversary. A special broadcast is scheduled for this Thursday, July 9. Details of the broadcast content are currently kept secret.

headline: UrLife E20 Electric Bike with 1,000W Peak and 80-Mile Range for $297
summary: The UrLife E20 adult electric bike is on sale for $297. It features a 1,000W peak motor and 48V battery. The bike offers a 28mph top speed and 80-mile range.

headline: The Elder Scrolls Online Reportedly Loses Half Its Development Team
summary: The Elder Scrolls Online development team has reportedly seen significant layoffs. Up to half of its staff have been affected. The game's future roadmap is now under re-evaluation.

headline: Trump Confirms Asking FIFA Boss for Review of US Striker's Ban
summary: Donald Trump confirms he asked FIFA for a review of Folarin Balogun's ban. He states FIFA "made the right decision" to suspend the ban. Trump claimed the ban would have "stained" the World Cup.

headline: Buckingham Palace: Prince Harry Will Not Stay There Despite Team's Announcement
summary: Buckingham Palace states Prince Harry will not stay at the Palace. This contradicts an earlier announcement by his team. Royal sources indicate Harry missed a deadline for accommodation offers.

headline: Britain's Arthur Fery Reaches Wimbledon Quarter-Finals in Historic Match
summary: Arthur Fery advances to the Wimbledon quarter-finals. He beat Grigor Dimitrov in a five-set thriller. Fery is the first British wildcard to reach a Grand Slam quarter-final.

headline: Consultants in England Vote in Favour of NHS Strikes
summary: Senior doctors in England have voted to strike. They now have a mandate for industrial action. Strikes could occur over the next 12 months in the NHS.
